reviews.review_title: Great Game, Needs Work, Hard to Progress
reviews.review_description:
Do not mistake my three star review for a reason not to try the game. If you enjoy turn based card games, similar to magic the gathering, this is a fantastic one. It brings the feeling of playing a card game to a 3D aspect where the creatures come to life.

The game has a great start. There are many interesting mechanics that make it different compared to other types of card games which makes it great. The game functions very similarly to Magic the Gathering. The mana system is easy to understand and overall the game isn't too complex. When building a deck, you decide a primary faction and a secondary faction, the deck is based around. The primary faction determines how the deck will run. There is Dungeon Master wild horde, plundering guild and Augur order. Dungeon master decks work by having one creature that is tied to your direct life force that has an ability that will affect every other creature you have on the field you can fight with your Dungeon Master and use him just like any other creature can be used and these decks typically focus on spells and undead creatures and necromancy. Wild horde decks work by moving creatures to your reserves where you can use spells and other creatures to mobilize your reserves for little to none or no mana cost at all. Plundering guild decks have you generate coins every turn or by using spells or creatures; these coins can be used to enhance specific cards to add additional abilities or make a card stronger. Unfortunately I've not played Augur Order so I cannot explain it. The game is interesting and is very enjoyable.

However, the progression is extremely slow. It takes forever to level up in the battle pass they have and getting specific packs requires gems and gems are extremely difficult to obtain because the only way you can obtain gems pretty much is by buying them or buying the pass or subscribing to their patron thing. Not to mention every cosmetic in the game is extremely overpriced. Getting one singular item typically costs 1000 gems, which is the equivalent of ten US dollars. If you were to buy a singular pair of gloves or a helmet or armor this would cost 1000 gems. 90% of the stuff is extremely overpriced for no good reason. I have a massive amount of coins that I can't use on anything except packs. But the problem is the packs I need cost gems. Because of the need for gems, it is difficult to fabricate good decks and it takes easily months to get all the cards you need. I can understand the fact that this is a free game and the developers need some sort of income. So it's reasonable to price cosmetics which aren't necessary for the game at a higher price than many would agree with. However, the deck packs that help you grow in the game should not have this cost for gems, unless they are animated packs which is reasonable. 

An additional problem is the moderation. I have not been a victim of poor moderation too much. However, the biggest complaint I know of is that the moderators don't deal with the people who are toxic and rude and have a tendency to ban you if you retaliate to the people, being toxic and rude.

Overall, the game is a very fun card game that feels like magic the gathering with 3D creatures and it has improved more and more, with fantastic updates an additions to the game, except It seems like the developers have gotten very greedy(even for the game being Free) and moderation needs improvement.

The Game:     

Super easy to play and provides incredible amounts of fun.

The Atmosphere:

If the T.V. show Cheers hosted a Magic the Gathering party.

The Content:

Plenty of content, especially if you're just starting out. 

The Community:

Everyone is willing to help, and the trouble makers are actually handled in appropriate ways.
 
The Cons:

Yesterday I heard a new person say, "I probably won't play this game because I don't like losing." 

So yea, it's that good.

If you're looking for a chill card game, this isn't it. Prohibitive process to even start the game, you'll need to create a separate account from your Meta account to even start the game. Then while playing the Tutorial card game against the computer, some kid gets too close, puts his hands in my face, and won't go away. Why do they allow spectators to interfere with people that are playing the card game? They have mods walking around for some reason that don't do anything and ask if anyone else saw it like you'd be lying. Just another den of screaming children.

The interactions between cards and the different factions can sometimes be complicated and lead to a lot of mid game reading to understand what’s happening but that can mostly be mitigated by using a rush down aggro deck.

Every person I meet on there is nice except for the moderators. I have met around 4 of them and they are absolutely never just a regular nice person.

reviews.review_title: I wish I liked playing this game
reviews.review_description:
Listen, I have never played MAGIC or anything like it. I didn't think I would enjoy this game all that much, but I was wrong. I really enjoy playing, and it's honestly one of the most consistent games I play. However, I have WAY many more complaints than I do praises. The progression system is awful. Far too slow for a 13+ game, (and I'm 22). You win barely any gold from battles and quests. There are some cards that are just straight up ridiculously OP and can't be beat without spell spamming. The moderation is abysmal. What's the point of Exhaulted servers if you can't swear in non-Exhaulted servers? It's a 13+ game, there is bound to be some language. The fact that someone can just report someone else for the littlest thing and the moderator won't even let you state your case is just victim-biased. I play with my friend who is also my age and we try to create new public lobbies to be by ourselves, (because obviously private lobbies are blocked by a paywall) and someone joins and starts pestering us. Last and certainly not least... the community. I have never met more egotistical, arrogant, snobs in my entire life. At first, I thought people were super helpful. When I had a question as a new player, almost the entire lobby would come to help. With time, I realized they just wanted to boast their superiority over the new players. Once they want to battle you, just know they aren't going to be so nice. I once battled some high ranking player who was sh!t talking me the entire time and when I won he threw a tantrum and reported me. 

TL;DR The game is good until you play for more than an hour because of the terrible progression, moderation, and community.
The game itself is very good but the community is way too small. It is basically MTG in vr.

Love this game! Love that I can play together with friends and build decks and customize them and customize my avatar. Collecting cards, building decks. Having fun with friends. It's a great game. Easy to learn.

I already been playing this game for around four years and I just want to say how much this game changed my life. 

I started out as a new player with absolutely no idea what I was doing than some people started helping me out with how to build decks and stuff, than immediately became friends with them.

After playing more I been making more friends and getting to know more people to the point where this game has been my hobby
I left for like a year but recently got back into the game and it still feels the same in a good way the updates are still fire. 

Can’t wait to see what the future of the game holds I love this game.

When you first start playing this game it's a blast there's quite a bit of stuff at your disposal to keep you playing. After the honeymoon phase is over and you realize that really only 3 decks get played in the meta constantly it gets very boring. When I started out as a new player higher ranked players would destroy me then jump up and down with joy like they won a tournament. If you try and play casually you get sweated on by a guy who thinks he's Albert Einstein. The only people who still currently play the game are all plats or higher so good luck playing against anyone but the ai or someone who's going to absolutely smoke you. ALSO the moderators act like they don't even wanna be there, almost like they are forced to get on at least once a week.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the detailed feedback, we really appreciate you taking the time to write this.

You’re absolutely right that at higher levels, the meta can feel narrow, especially when a smaller player population naturally concentrates around the strongest decks. We’re actively working on balance changes and new content specifically to widen viable deck choices and reduce repetitive matchups.

Matchmaking is another area we’re aware needs improvement. As an indie game with a limited player base, queues can skew toward higher-ranked players during off-peak times, which can make casual play feel punishing. We’re exploring adjustments to improve this experience and make matches feel fairer for newer and returning players.

Regarding player behavior: excessive sweating, poor sportsmanship, or harassment isn’t something we tolerate. If you ever encounter behavior that crosses the line, reporting that player help us address it.

As for moderation, our moderators are volunteers, and while they may not always be highly visible, they do review reports and help keep the community running. That said, we hear your feedback and are working on improving both communication and community presence.

Thanks again for playing and for being honest. Feedback like this directly helps shape where the game goes next.

If you're not skilled, don't complain about losing, just laugh it off. Even when it feels like something is unbeatable, remember that even the strongest of people have their weaknesses. There is always a counterplay, you just need to find it. Go enjoy the game if you arent already.

developer_response.body:
We’re sorry to hear about your frustration. We take moderation and fair play very seriously, and bans are never issued simply for beating a moderator or for personal reasons. All bans are based on evidence reviewed against our rules.

That said, we understand mistakes or misunderstandings can happen. If your friend believes the ban was incorrect, they’re welcome to submit an appeal so it can be reviewed by the team.

Ban appeals can be sent to:
support@divergent-realities.com

We appreciate feedback and are always working to improve transparency and fairness in our moderation process.
